contractSurface.securityClassification: tenant-scoped marketplace packaging, installation, review, usage metering, settlement, and sandbox controls
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-32 uses sharable-flows-marketplace as the canonical portability, UI, package, and runtime implementation slug.
coreGuarantees: Marketplace package records stay tenant scoped and content addressed.
Published versions are immutable; security updates create new versions.
Artifact signing, verification, certification, and notarization remain required before marketplace trust is declared.
Install and RAG blueprint sharing stay logic-plane only and must not copy tenant data, embeddings, documents, or secrets.
Binding documents store secret references only and never store literal credentials.
Governance migrations revalidate all consumers and do not sample.
Revenue settlement uses integer cents and idempotent settlement keys.
Fraud signals are routed to human review and cannot auto-suspend, ban, revoke, terminate, or disable a tenant.
Sandbox tenants use the sandbox prefix and remain isolated from production resources.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory for marketplace side effects.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-32 is a marketplace distribution surface; Tier 3 requires signing, provenance, and CI evidence before portability certification can advance.

services.className: Package Publication And Artifact Trust Pipeline
machineLockedRules: signing, verification, certification, and notarization cannot be bypassed
tenant data and embeddings are never copied between tenants
binding documents store only secret references
rollback paths distinguish migration failure from validation failure
RAG blueprint export, publish, and import remain logic-plane only
documents, embeddings, vector stores, tenant data, and secrets stay out of shared blueprints

services.className: Usage Revenue Fraud And Sandbox Pipeline
machineLockedRules: usage events preserve publisher and consumer tenant separation
settlement uses integer cents and idempotent period keys
fraud detection can flag and route but cannot auto-suspend
sandbox tenant identifiers keep the sandbox prefix

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-32 values may tune marketplace naming, curation, category presentation, permission prompts, dependency install behavior, discovery ranking, review thresholds, settlement cadence, and sandbox lifetime without changing tenant isolation, artifact trust, logic-plane sharing, secret-reference binding, human review, integer settlement, or store-before-event ordering.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to marketplace naming, category and curation presentation, install policy, dependency prompt behavior, discovery ranking, review threshold, settlement cadence, sandbox lifetime, and display metadata.

edgeCases.name: Acme curated flow store
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes the original package for a curated internal marketplace with stricter human review and short sandbox sessions.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: module naming, review SLA, category set, sandbox TTL
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Artifact trust, logic-plane sharing, tenant isolation, and human review remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind partner template exchange
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the adapted package for partner template discovery with weighted freshness and prompt-based dependency installs.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: discovery ranking, dependency install mode, permission policy
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Dependencies remain explicit tenant decisions and cross-tenant data copying remains prohibited.

edgeCases.name: Tessera community pattern library
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes the cascade package for a community pattern library with slower settlement and lower featured density.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: featured strip size, settlement period, fraud review threshold
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Revenue math stays integer-based and fraud outcomes still require human review.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T516 through T535 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1339 through F1418 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Marketplace package, install, usage, settlement, and sandbox records remain tenant scoped.
Artifact trust requires signing, verification, certification, and notarization evidence.
Install and RAG blueprint sharing remain logic-plane only.
Tenant data, embeddings, documents, vector stores, and literal secrets are not shared between tenants.
Binding documents store secret references only.
Revenue settlement uses integer cents only.
Fraud outcomes require human review and cannot auto-suspend, ban, revoke, terminate, or disable accounts.
Sandbox tenant IDs keep the sandbox prefix.
Store-before-event ordering and CloudEvents envelopes remain mandatory.

tenantACuratedFlowStore.description: Tenant A tunes Sharable Flows Marketplace for an internal curated flow store.
tenantACuratedFlowStore.impact: Tenant A gets a curated marketplace package while artifact trust, logic-plane sharing, secret-reference binding, tenant isolation, and human review stay unchanged.
tenantACuratedFlowStore.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-32 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-32 package assembly copies the sharable-flows-marketplace implementation under the canonical package slug path.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
